title: Using ML + GenAI to generate structured stock research — looking for feedback
author: mohit-1004
selftext:
Hi everyone,

I’m building **TradeThesis**, a platform that uses ML + GenAI to generate structured stock research.

When researching companies, I often end up jumping between financials, news, charts, and discussions across many tabs. The idea is to **bring all those signals into one structured “thesis-style” analysis** rather than just raw indicators.

Curious from people here:

• What signals do you rely on most?  
• Do you prefer raw indicators or summarized insights?

Would really appreciate feedback.  
[https://tradethesis.in](https://tradethesis.in)

comments.author: Jess_Parker19
comments.body: how do I merge ML with gen AI? my computer is too slow to run AI locally so I’ve gotta use a neural net

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Interesting idea — I definitely relate to the “15 tabs open” problem.

Personally, the signals I rely on most depend on the time horizon:

- **Long-term:** revenue growth consistency, margin trends, FCF generation, capital allocation history, and management commentary vs. actual execution.
- **Short/medium-term:** earnings revisions, guidance changes, sentiment shifts, unusual volume, and macro/sector tailwinds.

What I’ve found is that raw indicators are necessary, but only if they’re contextualized. For example, “revenue up 20%” is meaningless without knowing:
- Is that acceleration or deceleration?
- Organic or acquisition-driven?
- How does it compare to peers?

So I prefer **structured summaries with clear sourcing and traceability**. If GenAI generates a thesis, I’d want:
1. Explicit links to underlying data
2. Clear separation between facts vs. interpretation
3. Some signal of confidence/uncertainty

One concern with ML + GenAI in this space is hallucination or subtle misinterpretation of financial data. Even small inaccuracies can destroy trust. How are you validating outputs against filings?

Also, how customizable is the thesis structure? Different investors prioritize different frameworks (DCF-heavy vs. narrative-driven vs. factor-based).

If you can combine structured summaries + drill-down raw data + transparency in reasoning, that’d be compelling. Otherwise it risks becoming another high-level summary tool.

Curious how you’re differentiating from existing research aggregators or AI wrappers around filings.

comments.author: mohit-1004
comments.body:
Really appreciate the detailed feedback — this is super helpful.

I completely agree on the context vs raw numbers point. The direction with TradeThesis is to focus on structured reasoning around the data, not just metrics or generic summaries.

A few things you mentioned are exactly what I’m thinking about building in:
• clear separation between facts vs interpretation
• links to underlying sources (filings/data)
• ability to drill down into raw data from the thesis

On hallucinations, the plan is to keep the numerical layer deterministic (from structured financial data) and use GenAI mainly to organize and explain the context.

Still shaping how customizable the thesis frameworks should be, but feedback like this is really helpful while figuring that out.
